description: In 2011, British executive Michael Woodford was fired as CEO of Japan's Olympus Corporation after questioning $1.7 billion in unexplained payments. The case exposed deep-seated Japanese corporate governance failures and nepotism.

key_events.description: Michael Woodford is abruptly dismissed as CEO by the board.
Woodford leaks documents to the Financial Times exposing the cover-up.
Olympus stock price collapses by 80% as the scandal is confirmed.
Company admits to decades of accounting cover-ups involving 'tobashi' schemes.

calibration_notes: Highlights the 'hero whistleblower' dynamic where a single individual against a board creates an asymmetric narrative of institutional corruption.
